$schema_comment: Lightweight catalog of design profiles. design-analyst reads this first, then loads only the 1-3 candidates that match the user's request. Profiles are sorted from most-versatile (top) to most-specialized (bottom).

best_for: Marketing sites for premium SaaS or services
Brand sites where editorial tone matters
Login / signup pages that feel like a confident invitation
not_for: Dense product UIs (use linear-product or vercel-dev)
profiles.one_line: Warm-neutral palette, serif display + sans body. The 'looks like a designed product' tier.

best_for: Product surfaces (dashboards, admin UIs, in-app screens)
Modern SaaS where the product is the design, not marketing
not_for: Marketing landing pages for non-technical audiences (use editorial-premium or apple-marketing)
Pure dev tools — use vercel-dev for those
profiles.one_line: Monochromatic with one purple-blue accent. Inter throughout. Geometric, dense, precise.

best_for: Anything where monospace + pure BW + Geist signals 'made for developers'
not_for: Consumer audiences (cold palette will feel sterile)
Anything where warmth or playfulness is needed
profiles.one_line: Pure black/white, Geist Sans + Geist Mono. Developer-native by default.

best_for: Modern fintech products that want to look NOTHING like a 1990s bank
not_for: Consumer fintech for younger demographics (use playful-pastel)
profiles.one_line: Deep navy + cream + sage accent. Confident without security theater. Killed the fintech-blue stereotype.

best_for: Reading-heavy content surfaces that still need navigation
not_for: Long-form essays for general readers (use substack-warm)
profiles.one_line: Pure neutral, Inter throughout, sidebar + content layout. Readable, navigable, no flourish.
Soft pastels (peach, sky, sage) on cream. Hand-illustrated feel without literal illustrations. Warmth as a posture.

best_for: Marketing pages for polished consumer products (apps, hardware)
Premium consumer brands where Apple is the obvious reference
not_for: Editorial brand sites with warmth (use editorial-premium)
profiles.one_line: SF Pro family, monochrome with subtle product accents. Hero-driven, full-bleed sections, one focal point per fold.

best_for: Personal sites and portfolios for writers
not_for: Any product UI (totally wrong for app surfaces)
profiles.one_line: Warm off-white, serif display + serif body (yes, body serif here — reading first). Single column, big margins, no chrome.

best_for: Creative agency and design studio sites
Anywhere intentional rough edges signal taste
not_for: Anything users need to navigate quickly (brutalism prioritizes statement over efficiency)
profiles.one_line: Pure BW + one shocking primary. Mono OR oversized display sans. Asymmetric, sharp, deliberate.

inspired_by: Linear Loops (the brand site, not product)
best_for: Products that need a STRONG visual identity to stand out
AI-native products where the gradient is intentional brand, not template clutter
not_for: Anything where the brand needs to recede (editorial-premium fits better)
profiles.one_line: Signature gradient (intentional, not template) + sharp typography + bold hero moments. Identity-as-design.
